properties.name: Sambhar Salt Lake — Central Works
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + Salt Commissioner records

facts: India's largest inland salt lake — 22.5 km long, 3-11 km wide; ~230 sq km at full spread.
Sambhar Salts Ltd (a Hindustan Salts joint venture) is one of only two Government of India salt-producing PSUs.
Solar evaporation produces ~200,000 tonnes annually — 9% of India's salt output and India's largest source of inland (rock-and-brine) salt.
Also a Ramsar site (1990) — hosts Rajasthan's largest flamingo congregation during winter (~50,000 lesser and greater flamingoes).

properties.name: Didwana Salt Lake
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + Salt Commissioner records

facts: Rajasthan's second-largest saline lake after Sambhar; the Rajasthan State Chemical Works produces salt and sodium sulphate here.
The salt has a slightly higher sulphate content than Sambhar's, so Didwana salt is prized for the chemical industry rather than edible use.
Nawalgarh Salts co-operative is Rajasthan's oldest continuously-operating salt cooperative (1908).

properties.name: Pachpadra Salt Works
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + Salt Commissioner records

facts: Unique 'lana khar' method — sub-surface brine is drawn up by lowering thorny khejri branches into pits; salt crystallises around the twigs and is harvested manually.
The traditional method produces some of India's cleanest edible salt; Balotra's salt is prized for its low bitter-magnesium content.
Sits atop the Barmer petroleum basin; the same evaporite geology explains both the salt and the deep oil-and-gas reserves.

properties.name: Lunkaransar Salt Lake
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + Salt Commissioner records

facts: Northernmost saline lake in Rajasthan; salt is harvested only in dry monsoon years (highly variable).
The lake bed is a well-studied palaeoclimate record — sediment cores show 8,000+ years of monsoon variability.
Nearby IGNP command has diluted the natural salinity somewhat since 1980s; production has declined.

properties.name: Kuchaman Salt Works
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + Salt Commissioner records

facts: Small salt lake in the Nagaur belt — historically part of the same aquifer system as Didwana and Sambhar.
Kuchaman salt is sold to nearby chemical works in Bhiwadi and Alwar; not sold as edible salt.
Overshadowed by Sambhar and Didwana; still a functioning industrial salt source.
